source: Security Enhancements - Introduction to PAM and basic usage: The four facilities, module return states, and control flags

questions.q: What are the four PAM facilities (module types)?
questions.why: auth, account, session, password are the four facilities.

questions.q: What does the auth facility do?
options: Authenticates the applicant and establishes account credentials
Sets up and tears down sessions
questions.why: auth handles authentication; account handles non-auth availability (time/workload) checks.

questions.q: What does the password facility do?
options: Changes the authentication token (because it expired or the user wants to change it)
questions.why: password management updates the auth token.

questions.q: A PAM module returns one of which three states?
questions.why: success (meets policy), failure (does not), or ignore (not involved in the policy).

questions.q: What does the 'required' control flag mean?
options: The module must succeed, but failure is not reported to the user until all such modules in that interface have run
Success ends the chain at once
questions.why: required must pass; notification of failure is deferred until the chain finishes.

options: On failure, requisite stops and notifies immediately; required keeps running the rest of the chain first
questions.why: requisite fails fast; required defers the failure.

questions.q: What does the 'sufficient' control flag do?
options: If it succeeds (and no prior required module failed) the user is authenticated with no further modules needed; if it fails, its result is ignored
It must succeed or auth fails
It always ends the chain on failure
questions.why: A successful sufficient short-circuits to grant; a failing one is skipped over.

questions.q: How does the 'substack' control differ from 'include'?
options: Both pull in another file's lines, but with substack a requisite failure only ends the sub-stack (not the parent stack)
include isolates failures; substack does not
questions.why: substack nests a stack; a requisite failure inside it returns to the parent rather than killing it.
